description_long: This jersey barrier is a robust, rectangular structure with a predominantly black surface, featuring a distinctive blue diagonal stripe pattern along its length. The central section is indented with an angular, interlocking design, possibly for modular connection with other barriers. The surface appears to have a slightly glossy finish, with visible scuff marks and signs of wear, suggesting frequent use in urban environments. The ends of the barrier are solid and squared, while the top edge is mostly flat except for the central notch. The material looks like heavy-duty plastic or composite, possibly reinforced with concrete or metal for added mass and durability. The overall appearance is utilitarian, designed for effective access control and vehicle redirection in city streets or construction zones.
description: A black, rectangular jersey barrier with blue diagonal stripes, a central interlocking notch, and a slightly glossy, worn surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length, blue stripes, and central interlocking notch.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile and the squared end of the barrier.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with blue stripes and the central notch visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other squared end and the ribbed texture on the end face.
